title: Gemara — CALM Control Requirement Standard
description: Extends the CALM control-requirement schema with the fields a Gemara control catalog carries, so any Gemara catalog (CCC, OSPS, AIGF, ...) can be expressed as CALM controls without losing its source identity or grouping. Framework-agnostic: no per-catalog id formats or applicability enums.

gemara-control-id.description: Parent Gemara control id, verbatim (e.g. 'CCC.Core.CN01'). Traceability anchor back to the source catalog.

group.description: Control group from the source catalog (e.g. 'Encryption').

applicability.description: Applicability category ids as declared in the source catalog (e.g. CCC TLP levels, OSPS maturity levels).

recommendation.description: Implementation recommendation for the assessment requirement, if the source provides one.

state.description: Lifecycle state of the assessment requirement in the source catalog (e.g. 'Active', 'Deprecated', 'Retired'), so consumers can distinguish live obligations from superseded ones.

catalog-author.description: metadata.author.id of the source Gemara catalog (e.g. 'finos-ccc'). Carried independently of catalog-id because a registry may combine them (e.g. grc.store '{author}/{id}') and we cannot assume how.

catalog-id.description: metadata.id of the source Gemara catalog (e.g. 'ccc.marefarc.cn').

catalog-version.description: metadata.version of the source Gemara catalog release (e.g. 'v2026.06-rc1'). Together with catalog-author and catalog-id this pins the requirement to a specific released version — the traceability anchor a consuming tool resolves back to the source release.
